rationale: The citation is used for more than background listing. In the discussion, the authors cite Khan, Zhu, and Kalra (2011) as key prior evidence that higher construal reduces trade-off processing, and they position their own work relative to that mechanism. In Experiment 3, they explicitly rely on Khan et al. for the coding scheme used to identify attribute-level trade-off comparisons in participants' thought protocols, indicating methodological influence on core process testing.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Khan et al. (2011) as an important prior basis for the claim that construal level changes trade-off processing in choice. It builds on that basis in a different problem setting (assortment size and choice difficulty) and uses Khan et al.'s trade-off coding approach in its mechanism analysis.
